compute.note: US holds the large majority of frontier training compute; 700/1000 maps that dominance with room to grow or lose it

energy.note: grid adequate today, tightening: IEA has datacentre demand at 415 TWh in 2024 rising to ~945 TWh by 2030 (base case)

talent.note: strong but contested US lead in frontier researchers per the AI Index workforce chapters; index mapping is design judgment

capital.note: record private AI investment through 2025-26 per the AI Index; index mapping is design judgment

publicTrust.note: public-opinion surveys show majority unease about AI; below midpoint by design

capability.note: frontier systems handle hour-scale tasks reliably; the fog zone starts far above

safetyInsight.note: interpretability real but young; technical safeguards immature per the 2025 IAISR second update

jobDisplacement.note: measurable early displacement in exposed occupations, not yet macro-visible

unrest.note: background political tension, no AI-specific movement yet

capability.note: months behind the frontier, not years: DeepSeek-class efficiency closed much of the gap

trust.note: wary engagement: dialogue channels exist, verification does not

substitution.note: domestic chip substitution underway (Huawei/SMIC), progress real but constrained

startAllocation.note: mirrors the current revealed preference of the ecosystem: capability-heavy
